event: 66 years old
male
underwent colonoscopy
had no history of trauma or surgery
had DM
had obesity
consumed a bottle of rice wine thrice a week
15 polyps were resected by endoscopic mucosal resection
hot snare was used
preventive hemoclips were applied
nine polyps were located in the right colon
six polyps were located in the left colon and rectum
cecal intubation time was 3 minutes
quality of bowel preparation in all colon segments was excellent
discharged from the endoscopy room
visited the emergency department 24 hours after the procedure
complaining of right abdominal pain
tenderness and rebound tenderness were noted
white blood cell count was 21,460/mm3
C-reactive protein level was 17.8 mg/dL
blood urea nitrogen level was 28 mg/dL
creatinine was 2.13 mg/dL
lactic acid was 2.4 mmol/L
total bilirubin was 1.7 mg/dL
abdominopelvic computed tomography (CT) was performed
no pneumoperitoneum or bowel perforation was found
multiple air bubbles in the right lateral abdominal muscles were found
intensive medical treatments, including broad-spectrum antibiotic therapy were started
emergency exploratory laparotomy was performed
laparoscopic right hemicolectomy was performed
diagnosis of NF of the abdominal wall muscle was made
urgent surgical debridement and drainage were performed
despite surgical debridement, extensive broad-spectrum antibiotic therapy was given
best supportive care with renal replacement therapy in the intensive care unit was given
two more surgical debridements and drainages were performed
imipenem-resistant Acinetobacter baumannii and extended spectrum beta-lactamase negative Escherichia coli were identified
died because of septic shock and multiple-organ failure
Note: the time of death is estimated to be 2520 hours after the procedure, assuming the patient died 35 days after admission and each day has 24 hours.
